pattern: Memoized DFS or sort-by-height DP: indices can only be jumped to from higher bars, enabling a natural DAG order.
hints: Sort indices by arr value so you always process lower bars before higher ones; a bar can only be jumped from a taller bar.
For each index i (processed in increasing height order), scan up to d steps left and right; stop scanning in a direction as soon as you hit a bar >= arr[i].
dp[i] = 1 + max(dp[j]) over all reachable j; the answer is the maximum dp[i].

edge_inputs.description: all equal heights, no jumps possible
strictly decreasing, all reachable from index 0
blocked by intermediate bars when d is large
two elements, can jump from larger
valley surrounded by equal heights on both sides

stress.description: Random array with distinct values and random d
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    arr = rng.sample(range(1, 100001), size)
    d = rng.randint(1, size)
    return [arr, d]
